immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped heart-rate measurement event representing heart rate as an event-level observation rather than a baseline, summary, score, or stay-level aggregate.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_heart_rate must preserve this event-level heart-rate observation meaning.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: A later review may lock an exact or broader anchor after the cross-database observation boundary is frozen more formally.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_heart_rate from event-level raw measurement to baseline heart rate
changing std_heart_rate from raw measured values to averaged or summarized heart rate
changing std_heart_rate from heart rate observation to a derived rhythm or score concept
version_bump_trigger_examples: changing display precision while preserving the same event-level heart-rate meaning
adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same immutable semantic core
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing baseline heart rate as std_heart_rate
publishing stay-level averaged heart rate as std_heart_rate
mixing raw measured values with derived or aggregated values without explicit contract revision

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_heart_rate MVP execution should now go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_heart_rate outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
